sessions.title: FRIDAY EVENING | Slackline Yoga and AcroYoga w/YogaSlackers ($15.00 Additional Fee) (Green Space) 5:30PM-7:00PM
sessions.description: Kick off the festival FRIDAY NIGHT by exploring two exciting practices at once! This class offers you a chance to try both Slackline Yoga and AcroYoga. Two worlds that share the same love for balance, connection, and playfulness. Whether you're brand new or have some experience in one or both, this class is a great way to explore the foundations of each practice. You'll play on the slackline and explore partner movements on the ground, discovering how both cultivate trust, focus, and joy. Come connect, play, and dive into these unique practices! You might just find a new passion or deepen your understanding of both! You do not need a partner or prior experience to join this class.

sessions.title: FRIDAY EVENING| Outdoor Floating Sound bath w/Ananda Priti ($15.00 additional fee) (In the Pool) 6:00PM-7:00PM
sessions.description: Experience ultimate relaxation as you float in a serene pool on a comfortable raft, immersed in the soothing sounds of a continuous sound bath by Ananda Priti. This float will be OUTSIDE, weather permitting Ananda, a talented sound healer, creates a harmonious atmosphere with crystal singing bowls that resonate beautifully, promoting deep relaxation and inner peace. The gentle rocking of the water enhances this transformative journey of self-discovery and rejuvenation. Designed as a sanctuary for well-being, this event invites you to let go of stress and embrace the healing power of music and water. Floating rafts will be available for you to be assisted onto and off of. You'll receive a cozy blanket, a head pillow, and an eye mask for a fully immersive experience. (A lifeguard will be present to ensure your safety throughout.)

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* FRIDAY EVENING SUNSET | Outdoor Floating Sound bath w/Ananda Priti ($15.00 additional fee) (In the Pool) 7:30PM - 8:30PM
sessions.description: Imagine an evening of tranquility as the sun sets, transforming the serene pool into a magical oasis surrounded by flickering candle lights. Their soft glow dances on the water’s surface, enhancing the peaceful ambiance. Experience ultimate relaxation as you float on a comfortable raft, immersed in the soothing sounds of a continuous sound bath by Ananda Priti, with the air filled with a delicate fragrance of essential oils. Ananda, a talented sound healer, creates a harmonious atmosphere with crystal singing bowls, promoting deep relaxation and inner peace. The gentle rocking of the water and melodic vibrations help you drift into blissful tranquility. This sanctuary for well-being invites you to let go of stress and embrace the healing power of music and water. Floating rafts will be available for seamless boarding, and you’ll receive a cozy blanket, a head pillow, and an eye mask.

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* Grounded Grace: Beginner Aerial Restorative w/Nancy Forstbauer (Farrell Center Dance Studio) 8:00AM-9:00AM
sessions.description: Step into a calm, nurturing space where movement meets surrender. In this low silk, beginner-friendly aerial yoga class, Nancy guides you through simple, slow, and soft movements designed to restore balance and ease. The low-hanging silks offer gentle support, allowing you to explore trust, release tension, and connect deeply with your body. Each class invites you to honor your unique rhythm — there’s no rush, no expectation, just the freedom to listen to your intuition and make the practice truly yours. Perfect for all levels, especially those new to aerial yoga or seeking a mindful, restorative experience. This style of yoga is done in a soft fabric hammock elevated above the ground about 3 or 4 feet. In contrast to a traditional yoga class where we use strength to hold our poses. Aerial yoga is about learning how to let go within the posture. The hammock is used to make inversions more accessible by alleviating back and neck compression. It also aligns the spine and decompresses the joints of the body.

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* Ebb & Flow w/Festival Coordinator Raegan Reed (Long Field A) 8:00AM-9:00AM
sessions.description: By definition, ebb and flow means; a recurrent or rhythmical pattern of coming and going or decline and regrowth. This expression makes reference to the regular movement of the tides, where ebb means move away from the land and flow move back towards it. Like an ocean wave, we will start low and slow, we will pick up momentum midway and we will flow back down. In this class, you will experience twists, turns, deep stretching, a deep focus on alignment and breath, yummy hands on adjustments and much more! All levels welcome and encouraged! Modifications will be offered and everyone can move at their own pace.

sessions.title: Intro to Meditation w/Melissa Boyd (Middlefield Nook) 8:00AM - 9:00AM
sessions.description: In this transformative workshop you will learn all of the basic techniques to learning meditation. There are two broad categories of meditation: guided visualizations and focal point concentration meditations. All of the meditations fall into these broad groups: from zen to loving kindness, to vipassana to mantra, and music meditations. In the workshop we will explore a few types of meditations finding the techniques to calm the body, silence the mind, and enrich the soul.

sessions.title: Slackline Yoga and AcroYoga w/YogaSlackers (Green Space) ($15.00 Additional Fee) 8:00AM-9:00AM
sessions.description: Kick off the festival by exploring two exciting practices at once! This class offers you a chance to try both Slackline Yoga and AcroYoga. Two worlds that share the same love for balance, connection, and playfulness. Whether you're brand new or have some experience in one or both, this class is a great way to explore the foundations of each practice. You'll play on the slackline and explore partner movements on the ground, discovering how both cultivate trust, focus, and joy. Come connect, play, and dive into these unique practices! You might just find a new passion or deepen your understanding of both! You do not need a partner or prior experience to join this class.

sessions.title: Ashtanga Led Primary Series w/Bibi Lorenzetti (Long Field B) 8:00AM-9:00AM
sessions.description: Join Bibi Lorenzetti, authorized level II Ashtanga Yoga teacher for a traditional led primary series class. Bibi will guide students through the full Primary Series to the rhythm of the traditional Sanskrit count. Variations will be offered for certain postures. The practice includes two types of sun salutations, standing postures, seated postures, back bends, finishing postures, and breathing. Previous experience necessary.

sessions.title: Outdoor Floating Sound bath w/Ananda Priti ($15.00 additional fee) (In the Pool) 9:30AM-10:20AM
sessions.description: Experience ultimate relaxation as you float in a serene pool on a comfortable raft, immersed in the soothing sounds of a continuous sound bath by Ananda Priti. This float will be OUTSIDE, weather permitting. Ananda, a talented sound healer, creates a harmonious atmosphere with crystal singing bowls that resonate beautifully, promoting deep relaxation and inner peace. The gentle rocking of the water enhances this transformative journey of self-discovery and rejuvenation. Designed as a sanctuary for well-being, this event invites you to let go of stress and embrace the healing power of music and water. Floating rafts will be available for you to be assisted onto and off of. You'll receive a cozy blanket, a head pillow, and an eye mask for a fully immersive experience. (A lifeguard will be present to ensure your safety throughout.)

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* 60-minute Yoga Trapeze w/Nancy Forstbauer (Farrell Center Dance Studio) 9:30AM - 10:30AM
sessions.description: In this 60- minute class you will experience invigorating, powerful and safe movements. It is a combination of resistance training and yoga with a robust mix of inversions and deep stretches while being supported throughout all poses. Students have described it as non-stop fun with empowering results. Reported benefits include relief of back pain, core strength development, deeper backbends and more shoulder rotation. Additional benefits of Yoga Trapeze are increased Flexibility, Mobility, Balance and Strength. Through a series of poses you are able to build a strong core, develop upper and lower body strength and move into safe backbends and inversions using functional strength. Functional strength movements include pulling, holding and pushing using your body weight. This class will leave you floating on air!

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* Heart-Wide-Open Vinyasa Flow w/Selena Reynolds (Long Field A) 9:30AM-10:30AM
sessions.description: Join Selena for a heart-opening, energizing vinyasa flow. In yoga, we strive to remove any obstacles or blockages that prevent us from accessing our innate freedom of movement and being. This class will help release tension in the neck and shoulders and open the front body to move us into back bending postures with greater ease and joy. We will also learn how to strengthen the back body and use the core muscles to protect the lower back. Backbends are natural mood-lifters! Backbends also help us to access the full expression of our energy and vitality by breathing more fully! Come get heart-happier and sustainably back-bendier!!

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* TRAMPOLINE FUN - *JUMPSTART* Your Day with Bounce Basics w/ Sabrina Ursaner (Middlefield Nook) 9:30AM-10:30AM
sessions.description: *SPACE IS LIMITED! Sweat it out, bounce & dance to the beat, and JUMPstart your Catskill Mountain Yoga Festival experience with this amazingly fun cardio rebounding class on Jumpsport mini fitness trampolines. Rebounding classes are a great way to build muscle, balance, and endurance, all without hurting your joints: moderate-to-high-intensity with little-to-no impact! Plus they're so much fun you'll barely realize it's a full body workout. We are so excited to be offering trampoline classes this year. Learn form and technique, sweat a little, have FUN, and let go of whatever is weighing you down to soar to new heights in this exhilarating session suitable for beginners and ALL levels, including if this is your first time ever setting foot on a trampoline. *Space is extremely limited for this class. Be sure to register early.

sessions.title: Hike w/Gary Leahy, Semi-Strenuous (Meet at picnic tables in Vendor Village) 9:30AM-10:30AM
sessions.description: Join Gary Leahy as we hike UP to the TOP of the Mountain behind SUNY Delhi. This hike will take 60 minutes to get up the mountain. From there, you can hike back down. Please wear sunscreen, sneakers and bring water.
sessions.location: Meet at picnic tables in Vendor Village

sessions.title: EcoPrinting w/Marguerite Uhlmann-Bower (Meet at picnic tables near Vendor Village) 9:30AM -10:30AM
sessions.description: Eco-Printing: A natural technique that honors Natures cycles by transferring plant and tree pigments onto paper or fabric. Learn a little. Harvest some. Co-create a lot. Take home surprising, healing, relatable plant patterns.
sessions.location: Meet at picnic tables near Vendor Village

sessions.title: Outdoor Floating Sound bath w/Ananda Priti ($15.00 additional fee) (In the Pool) 11:00AM-11:50AM
sessions.description: Experience ultimate relaxation as you float in a serene pool on a comfortable raft, immersed in the soothing sounds of a continuous sound bath by Ananda Priti. This float will be OUTSIDE, weather permitting. Ananda, a talented sound healer, creates a harmonious atmosphere with crystal singing bowls that resonate beautifully, promoting deep relaxation and inner peace. The gentle rocking of the water enhances this transformative journey of self-discovery and rejuvenation. Designed as a sanctuary for well-being, this event invites you to let go of stress and embrace the healing power of music and water. Floating rafts will be available for you to be assisted onto and off of. You'll receive a cozy blanket, a head pillow, and an eye mask for a fully immersive experience. (A lifeguard will be present to ensure your safety throughout.)

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* 60-minute Yoga Trapeze w/Nancy Forstbauer (Farrell Center Dance Studio) 11:00AM - 12:00PM
sessions.description: In this 60- minute class you will experience invigorating, powerful and safe movements. It is a combination of resistance training and yoga with a robust mix of inversions and deep stretches while being supported throughout all poses. Students have described it as non-stop fun with empowering results. Reported benefits include relief of back pain, core strength development, deeper backbends and more shoulder rotation. Additional benefits of Yoga Trapeze are increased Flexibility, Mobility, Balance and Strength. Through a series of poses you are able to build a strong core, develop upper and lower body strength and move into safe backbends and inversions using functional strength. Functional strength movements include pulling, holding and pushing using your body weight. This class will leave you floating on air!

sessions.title: My First AcroYoga Flow w/YogaSlackers (Middlefield Nook) 11:00AM-12:00PM
sessions.description: In this class, we’ll start with individual AcroYoga poses. Beautiful on their own shapes that help you feel stable and connected. Then, we’ll piece them together, moving from one pose to the next without touching the ground! The goal is to create a flying flowing “vinyasa” between three people: a base, a flyer, and a spotter. You’ll learn how these transitions can feel smooth and playful, and how working with others can take your practice to new heights. Come solo or with a friend with the intention to play, have fun and elevate each other. While no experience is necessary, it helps if you can hold a plank pose for 30 seconds.

sessions.title: Yin Yoga and Mythology w/Kelly Kamm (Long Field B) 11:00AM-12:00PM
sessions.description: Mythology has long been used to reflect the universal struggles of humankind. These folklore stories, told by and through relatable characters, help us feel more grounded and connected, so we can move through difficulties with grace, make friends with discomfort, and hold our center through uncertainty. Yin Yoga and Mythology combines rich storytelling traditions with powerful practices that are designed to offer physical, emotional, and mental relief, leaving you feeling ready to face the challenges of life with greater tenderness and ease.

sessions.title: abunDANCE Movement Meditation Manifestation Practice w/Isha Justyna Kostek (Long Field A) 11:00AM-12:00PM
sessions.description: abunDANCE is a 60-minute movement meditation that guides you through the 7 Rhythms of Abundance: release, gratitude, joy, compassion, love, peace, and abundance. Through breath, music, and free-form dance, you embody each rhythm to clear blocks and awaken your natural flow. The practice is both grounding and uplifting, leaving you open, radiant, and aligned with manifestation.

sessions.title: Essential Oils 101 w/Dr Victoria Ciummo (Meet at picnic tables in Vendor Village) 11:00AM-12:00PM
sessions.description: Victoria is a Doctor of Pharmacy and Wellness Consultant, who believes in a holistic approach to healing. She has a passion for helping others find healthy, natural alternatives focusing on the benefits of plants and essential oils. An introductory aromatherapy class educating on what essential oils are and how to use them. Workshop includes information on the therapeutic benefits, and sampling, of common essential oils; and optional guided breath work meditation.
sessions.location: Meet at picnic tables in Vendor Village

sessions.title: Vāsi Yoga: Understanding the Yogic Meditation State** *Ancient Siddha Breathwork, Modern EEG Insight, and the Biology of Inner Stillness w/Harish Bharadwaj (Clark Field House) 11:00AM-12:00PM
sessions.description: Ancient Siddha Breathwork, Modern EEG Insight, and the Biology of Inner Stillness After more than ten years of study under the Siddha lineage, Harish Bharadhwaj began asking a deeper question: what is actually happening in the body and brain when meditation becomes effortless? His breakthrough came through understanding the biology of meditation, how breath, awareness, nervous system regulation, and brainwave states work together to create the conditions for deep inner stillness. In this talk, Harish shares the foundation of Vāsi Yoga and his own EEG findings on the meditative state: what happens in the brain when stillness begins, and how this ancient Siddha breathwork practice can help sincere seekers move beyond surface relaxation toward a deeper, more stable meditation practice. In this session: Learn why meditation is not something you force, but a state the body enters when the right conditions are created. See how breath, stillness, and brainwave patterns reveal the shift from effort to meditation. Understand how Vāsi Yoga prepares the body and nervous system for a deeper, more stable meditative state.

sessions.title: Outdoor Floating Sound bath w/Ananda Priti ($15.00 additional fee) (In the Pool) 1:00PM-1:50PM
sessions.description: Experience ultimate relaxation as you float in a serene pool on a comfortable raft, immersed in the soothing sounds of a continuous sound bath by Ananda Priti. This float will be OUTSIDE, weather permitting. Ananda, a talented sound healer, creates a harmonious atmosphere with crystal singing bowls that resonate beautifully, promoting deep relaxation and inner peace. The gentle rocking of the water enhances this transformative journey of self-discovery and rejuvenation. Designed as a sanctuary for well-being, this event invites you to let go of stress and embrace the healing power of music and water. Floating rafts will be available for you to be assisted onto and off of. You'll receive a cozy blanket, a head pillow, and an eye mask for a fully immersive experience. (A lifeguard will be present to ensure your safety throughout.)

sessions.title: Plant Walk & Identification w/Marguerite Uhlmann-Bower (Meet in Clark Parking Lot) 1:00PM - 2:00PM
sessions.description: "Reclaiming Ancient Wisdom for Modern Times" w/Marguerite Uhlmann-Bower. Join the Radical Herbalist, Earth Medicine Walker and retired nurse for an informing outdoors plant walk in one’s self-help journey. Re-learn associations and patterns that radically change foraging methods which also help us remember our TEK’ie skill-set in a relational re-languaging way. Learn about key sacred and everyday Plants as helpers and healers. Food, medicine, ritual.

sessions.title: Iyengar Vinyasa w/Melissa Boyd (Long Field A) 1:00PM-2:00PM
sessions.description: Iyengar Vinyasa is a beautiful form of yoga that explores the form, technique and alignment of B.K.S.Iyengar in a smooth and seamless flow. There is a mindfulness of anatomy and injury prevention with each intricate flow. There are props there to assist you in your form, alignment, for your comfort level. The class is taught with precision, and knowledge to work on strengthening yourself physically in the poses, and mastering your mindset with philosophic lore. Each flow there is emphasis on becoming one with your Prana and being present. The class ends with a deep meditation to soothe your being. The class feels cathartic in nature emotionally and you feel complete in your being.

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* Soul Restorative Yoga w/Sabrina Elmaliah (Long Field B) 1:00PM-2:00PM
sessions.description: Come to rest and restore from the hustle of modern life, weary traveler. Your next steps to bliss begin with deep, long stretching for a melting like butter on pancakes, stress-relieving experience. Together we will weave detoxifying poses with ancient yogic wisdoms for a revitalizing, fulfilling class, that brings participants into clear tranquility and communion. Reset & return to your life feeling supported and blissful!

sessions.title: Fascia Fire: A Master Vinyasa Flow Class in Unlocking the Power of Your Fascia w/Kat Suda (Middlefield Nook) 1:00PM-2:00PM
sessions.description: A challenging and energizing power vinyasa class designed to target a specific myofascial meridian. Fascia is the connective tissue that both connects and separates EVERYTHING in the body. Within the fascial network are specific fascia lines. This practice will be sequenced and tuned to target a set of lines. The sequence will be designed to build heat, create hydration within the tissues, enhance stability, and ending with holds to enhance mobility and flexibility. This class will take you on a journey that not only challenges your strength and endurance, but also nourishes your body's connective tissue, increases range of motion, releases stored tension, all aiming to create a deeper freedom in your practice! Get ready to sweat, strengthen, and restore in this powerful flow! This workshop is open to all who are ready to dive deep and move with intention. Through challenges we grow, and through growth, we live!

sessions.title: Sun Salutations: Unveiling Perfectly Balanced Inversions | w/YogaSlackers (Farrell Center Dance Studio) 1:00PM-2:00PM
sessions.description: All Levels Welcome! What if your handstand practice could live inside your Sun Salutations? In this workshop, we reframe Surya Namaskar as a handstand lab: using familiar shapes to build the real ingredients of balance: shoulder elevation, core integration, weight transfer, and calm, steady breath. We’ll break the sequence into clear, repeatable micro-drills to alignment, strength, and confidence. You’ll learn how to layer intention into every transition, turning a warm-up into a purposeful path toward straighter, quieter entries and longer holds over time. No handstand experience needed. You’ll leave with a simple take-home protocol you can add to any practices stepping closer to your handstand goals, one Sun Salutation at a time.

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* SUP (Stand Up Paddle Board) Yoga w/Jessie Murray ($15 additional fee) (In the Pond) 2:30PM - 3:20PM
sessions.description: SUP (stand up paddleboard) Yoga takes our practice off of our mats and out into the elements of nature. Moving our practice out onto the board on the water provides a higher level of mindfulness, of presence, and a deeper understanding and connection with the world around us. The paddle is short, the 30 minute yoga flow is deep, and the short reflective paddle back brings a feeling that will stay with you long after you come back to land. All classes are designed with all practitioners in mind, and no experience in paddling or yoga is necessary. All gear is provided and all that is required is your presence and an open heard and mind.

sessions.title: Crow: The Groundwork Toward Handstands w/YogaSlackers (Farrell Center Dance Studio) 2:30PM-3:30PM
sessions.description: Understanding crow pose and other arm balance poses can help us build a solid foundation for handstands and other inversions. The secret starts the moment your hands touch the ground. In this class, we’ll decodify crow pose and other shapes to build the strength, alignment, and confidence for your future handstand journey. We’ll break movements down step-by-step to help you stabilize through hands and wrists, shift weight with control, and breathe with ease. If you’ve avoided arm balances due to pain or discomfort, we’ll focus on joint-friendly setup, smart technique, and personalized options so you can practice without strain. You’ll leave with a clear path to progress and a few drills you can repeat at home. All levels welcome. Come start (or restart) your balance journey.

sessions.title: Solar Invocation: The Fire of Becoming ; Kundalini style flow class | Breathwork | Mantra w/Sarah Brianna (Long Field A) 2:30PM-3:30PM
sessions.description: A ritual of empowerment for the solar plexus… Your inner sun. We’ll work with Ayurvedic fire (agni), solar breath, and heart-forward flow to awaken confidence, willpower, and creative radiance. Expect candlelight, breath of fire, and an invocation to Surya… The source of illumination within and without.

sessions.title: Mythical Vinyasa Master Class w/Kelly Kamm (Middlefield Nook) 2:30PM-3:30PM
sessions.description: Join Kelly Kamm for a spirited journey through Hindu myths, as well as the mantras, mudras and poses that go with them. Myths are like prisms, reflecting us back to ourselves and providing meaningful clues to help us through challenging moments in our lives. Coupled with creative vinyasa flow, the stories truly come to life with YOU as the main character! Krishna, Lakshmi, Kali, Shiva, Ganesha… any one of them (or more than one) might make an appearance as we flow through creative vinyasa sequences designed to unleash your inner deity! For all levels, this workshop includes storytelling, chanting, mudra (yoga for your hands), meditation and lots of inspired movement on the mat.

sessions.title: In the Middle Lies the Magic: A Nervous System–Informed Approach to Growth and Healing w/Emily Tomlinson (Long Field B) 2:30PM-3:30PM
sessions.description: Find your center where strength meets softness and challenge meets support. Through mindful movement and breath, we’ll explore how “teaching to the middle” supports physical alignment and nervous system harmony. Rooted in both anatomy and awareness, this approach invites every practitioner to leave feeling grounded, open, and connected to their capacity for growth and healing.

sessions.title: Raja Yoga Meditation w/Susan Pollock (Clark Field House) 2:30PM-3:30PM
sessions.description: Raja Yoga Meditation is practiced with the eyes open. This allows us to bring the benefits into our practical lives as we face people and situations with a calm and stable presence. There are no mantras, postures, or breathing exercises. In silence, we go deep within and connect with our own source of peace, love, joy, and wisdom. We learn to make our mind our best friend and harness its power. This brings about newness in the way we think about ourselves and the world we live in. During this session, we will begin the journey of learning the meditation and spiritual understanding of Raja Yoga. Through guided reflection and meditation, participants will be invited to tap into their inner reservoir of fresh energy and leave with practical methods they can apply in everyday life.

sessions.title: SUP (Stand Up Paddle Board) Yoga w/Jessie Murray ($15.00 additional fee) (In the Pond) 4:00PM - 4:50PM
sessions.description: SUP (stand up paddleboard) Yoga takes our practice off of our mats and out into the elements of nature. Moving our practice out onto the board on the water provides a higher level of mindfulness, of presence, and a deeper understanding and connection with the world around us. The paddle is short, the 30 minute yoga flow is deep, and the short reflective paddle back brings a feeling that will stay with you long after you come back to land. All classes are designed with all practitioners in mind, and no experience in paddling or yoga is necessary. All gear is provided and all that is required is your presence and an open heard and mind.

sessions.title: Rollin' with my FOAMIES: Foam Rolling and Self-Myofascial Release w/Sabrina Ursaner (Long Field B) 4:00PM-5:00PM
sessions.description: Rolling with my FOAMIES: Unwind and reset in this soothing class using foam rolling* techniques to release tension, ease muscle soreness, and promote deep relaxation. Through mindful movements and gentle pressure, you'll experience a full-body reset that calms the nervous system and melts away stress. Ideal for anyone needing relief from tightness or looking to simply relax, this session will leave you feeling refreshed, restored, and ready for a peaceful evening ahead. No experience whatsoever is needed. *Rollga® foam rollers will be provided for use during this class

sessions.title: Transformational Breathwork w/Ananda Priti (Middlefield Nook) 4:00PM-5:00PM
sessions.description: Ready for a transformative journey? Join Ananda Priti, an expert in Transformational Breathwork, and discover the healing power of your breath. As you breathe deeply, you’ll nourish your body, uplift your spirit, and enhance your mental clarity. Feel the rush of oxygen revitalizing your cells and promoting holistic health! You'll gain insights into the therapeutic benefits of breathwork and receive step-by-step guidance on this life-changing technique. Rest assured, you’ll be supported as you explore your inner landscapes and uncover patterns contributing to emotional or physical challenges. This breathwork practice helps release stored tension, emotions, and trauma, activating your body's natural healing abilities. Embrace this opportunity to unlock a healthier, more vibrant you through the transformative power of your breath!

sessions.title: *SOLD OUT* Rewire Your Inner World: A Hypnotic Reset for Stress, Anxiety & Self-Doubt w/Lisa Wisely (Clark Field House Classroom) 4:00PM-5:00PM
sessions.description: Rewire Your Inner World: A Hypnotic Reset for Stress, Anxiety & Self-Doubt with Lisa Wisely, Clinical Hypnotherapist & Coach. Anxiety doesn’t start in your body. It starts in your mind—and what’s been learned… can be unlearned. In this transformational one-hour workshop, you’ll discover how to calm the chaos, reset your nervous system, and rewire the thought patterns that keep you stuck in stress. Whether your anxiety is loud or quietly running in the background, you’ll leave with a new sense of control—and a deeper connection to your calmest self. Lisa will guide you through: The real reason anxiety feels automatic—and how to break the cycle The biggest thinking traps that fuel stress (and how to reframe them) Information on how your thoughts shape your emotions, habits, and even your health Quick brain-rewiring tools to shift from panic to peace—anytime, anywhere A soothing, empowering group hypnosis session to help you release anxiety and reconnect with clarity, confidence, and calm No experience with hypnosis needed—just bring your breath and an open mind. You’ll leave lighter. Grounded. And curious about what else is possible when you train your mind to work with you instead of against you.

sessions.title: My Very First Handstand w/YogaSlackers (Farrell Center Dance Studio) 4:00PM-5:00PM
sessions.description: Ready for a thrilling step into the world of handstands? By the end of this beginner-friendly class, you’ll be able to get into your very first handstand! We’ll break down the essentials (alignment, strength, and entry techniques) to help you safely and confidently get upside down. Whether handstands have always been a dream or a curious challenge, this workshop offers a supportive, step-by-step path. Pre-Req: You should be able to hold a 15-second plank pose and have no conditions that might make inversions unsafe. For example, high blood pressure or glaucoma.

sessions.title: Strength, Mobility & Alignment Yoga Flow w/Diane LaVon (Long Field A) 4:00PM-5:00PM
sessions.description: Students should be prepared to improve their mind body connection in their practice. Diane uses her certificates in personal training, yoga, pilates, and mobility to help students build a better awareness of form in yoga poses, and help students deepen their understanding of each posture. This class will help students build confidence in their practice while practicing drills that build strength and improve alignment.

sessions.title: FREE with Yogi Admission | Closing the Portal: Sound Bath Journey w/Jennifer Carlin (Long Field A) 5:30PM-6:30PM
sessions.description: The Grand Finale and Closing Ceremony! Join us for this grand finale, where we’ll honor the journey of the Catskill Mountain Yoga Festival with a celebration of movement, sound, and inner stillness. We will close out the Catskill Mountain Yoga Festival with a transformative journey through sound and stillness. we’ll gather for a restorative Sound Bath. Rest and receive the vibrations of healing sound, deepen your connection to the land and the elements, and create space for the wisdom and inspiration of the day to take root. Leave feeling nourished, clear, and connected. Everyone welcome!

sessions.title: SUNDAY MORNING | Get Your Handstands Off the Wall | Handstands for All | w/YogaSlackers (at The Cardio Club) 10:00AM-11:00AM
sessions.description: Located at The Cardio Club | 17 Elm Street\, Delhi\, NY 13753 To step away from the wall, you first need to truly know how to use it. In this workshop, we’ll teach you the skills that allow you to leverage the wall as a tool - not a crutch - and then guide you to move away from it as soon and as safely as possible. We’ll refine your entries, alignment, and balance points on the wall, while building the confidence and body awareness needed to balance freely in the center of the room. With clear progressions and the YogaSlackers’ 12 Days of Handstands as inspiration, you’ll learn the path towards standing on your hands without wall support. You’ll leave with the tools for a free handstand practice and forward progress. Pre-Req: Ability to hold a 15-second handstand against the wall or with a spotter.
